expect: Replacing full-tree git status in task_order artifact discovery will cut build_task_list wall time by at least 10s without changing surfaced artifact tasks.
actual: task_order.get_untracked_artifacts no longer uses full-tree git status. The targeted ls-files query reduced that step from 19.94s to 0.37s while still surfacing the same artifact class (untracked lessons/experiments). Full build_task_list timing remained noisy under live concurrent churn: 66.52s pre-patch in a profiled run versus 124.56s on a later live rerun, with remaining cost dominated by check_preemption (41.36s), dispatch tasks (19.09s), and maintenance-derived due parsing (9.08s). task_order.py --top 8 completed successfully in 126.5s on the patched run after earlier shell-level timeouts above 180s.
diff: Expected an end-to-end build_task_list reduction of at least 10s. That prediction was NOT confirmed on the live repo because concurrent session movement shifted the bottleneck after the artifact-discovery fix landed. The local hotspot prediction WAS confirmed strongly: artifact discovery improved by about 98 percent (19.94s -> 0.37s) without changing task type surfaced. Successor work should target check_preemption and dispatch/maintenance subprocess cost instead of artifact discovery.
